summary: Predict protein secondary structure (H/E/C) from amino acid sequence using residue propensity tables and sliding window nucleation.
preconditions: Propensity values > 1.0 indicate preference for that structure type
Nucleation-extension model: find seed regions first, then grow them
strategy: Build propensity lookup tables for helix and sheet from known values, then scan with sliding windows for nucleation sites
Extend nucleation regions along the sequence while average propensity exceeds threshold
Assign remaining unassigned residues as coil (C) and compute per-sequence composition statistics
AVOID: Window size matters: helix nucleation uses 6-residue windows, sheet uses 5-residue windows
AVOID: Handle sequence edges where window extends beyond bounds by padding or truncating
